articles.headline: India Steps Up Amidst Domestic Supply Concerns
articles.summary: India has stepped up purchases of Russian and Iranian oil and LPG, amidst domestic supply concerns. This marks India's first public acknowledgement of resuming energy trade with Iran, with Iranian volumes also starting to lift imports from Iran.

articles.headline: Tata Trusts Clarifies No Religious Bar on Trustees
articles.summary: Tata Trusts stated in an internal note that there is no legal bar on non-Zoroastrians serving as trustees, citing a 2000 legal opinion, following questions raised about Venu Srinivasan and Vijay Singh's eligibility.

articles.headline: India in Mission Mode to Secure Fertiliser Supplies
articles.summary: India is in talks with Russia, Morocco, Egypt, and other countries to secure vital fertiliser supplies amidst the West Asia conflict disrupting shipments and ahead of the kharif crop planning season.

articles.headline: Tier 2/3 Engineering Colleges Beat IT Hiring Slump
articles.summary: Despite a slowdown in entry-level IT hiring, several Tier 2/3 engineering colleges are seeing a better-than-expected placement season for the class of 2026, with more offers than last year.

articles.headline: Grey Market: Startups Bridge Gap in India's Ageing Population
articles.summary: A new wave of age-tech startups is emerging in India to address the needs of a rapidly ageing population, focusing on cognitive health, community networks, and senior-focused consumer products.

articles.headline: Five European FMs Push Bloc to Tax Windfall Profits
articles.summary: Five European Union finance ministers are calling for a tax on windfall profits of energy companies to ease the burden on the general public due to rising fuel prices.

articles.headline: OFS switch erodes ₹95,000 crore of investor wealth
articles.summary: Over 60% of fully offer-for-sale (OFS) IPOs from 2021-2026 are trading below their listing price. Promoters and early backers raised ₹1.76 lakh crore through these issues, which did not provide fresh capital for business growth. The trend has resulted in the destruction of approximately ₹95,000 crore in investor wealth as expensive valuations are being repriced during market corrections.

articles.headline: Venu Srinivasan resigns as trustee of Tata charitable institution
articles.summary: TVS Motor Company Chairman Emeritus Venu Srinivasan has resigned from the Bai Hirabai Jamsetji Tata Navsari Charitable Institution citing business commitments. The resignation follows a legal challenge by former trustee Mehli Mistry regarding eligibility criteria under the trust deed, including Parsi Zoroastrian faith and Mumbai residency requirements.

articles.headline: Stock market daily turnover hits 19-month high in March
articles.summary: Average daily turnover (ADT) in the cash equity market hit a 19-month high in March despite high volatility driven by the West Asia war. NSE cash market ADT jumped 10% to ₹1.25 lakh crore as retail investors used sharp dips to average their investments. However, benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty recorded their worst monthly performance since the pandemic, falling 10%.

articles.headline: Tech Call: Reliance Industries rated as long-term buy
articles.summary: Technical analysis of Reliance Industries suggests a bullish long-term outlook. With the stock at ₹1,351, analysts identify supports at ₹1,270 and ₹1,120. A break above the ₹1,610 resistance could take the price to ₹2,000 initially, with a five-year potential target of ₹6,000.

articles.headline: Five essential factors for bottom-fishing in volatile markets
articles.summary: With Indian indices down 15% due to US-Iran hostilities, investors are advised to focus on five factors when bargain-hunting. These include identifying structural resets in oil and inflation, avoiding small and micro-caps that suffer more in bear phases, staying clear of sectors that tanked most recently, and staggering entries rather than deploying all cash at once.

articles.headline: Vested introduces SIP options for global investment funds
articles.summary: FINTECH platform Vested has launched systematic investment plans (SIPs) for global funds. This allows Indian investors to invest fixed amounts at regular intervals into strategies managed by global asset managers such as Fidelity, Franklin Templeton, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan. The move facilitates easier access to international equity markets.

articles.headline: Travel insurance policies exclude most war-related claim scenarios
articles.summary: Travelers are cautioned that standard insurance policies typically exclude claims for trip cancellations or medical expenses directly caused by war or conflict-related attacks. While medical emergencies and baggage loss are covered under normal circumstances, flight disruptions due to drones or airport closures in conflict zones generally require recourse through airlines.

articles.headline: Investors advised to exit underperforming UTI Large Cap Fund
articles.summary: UTI Large Cap Fund (UTI Master Share) has seen lukewarm returns for years, underperforming benchmarks like the Nifty 100 TRI by 1-2.5 percentage points across various timeframes. Analysts suggest investors exit the fund and stop existing SIPs in favor of better-performing large-cap schemes, noting its struggle to beat peers even on a 10-year rolling return basis.

articles.headline: Zydus Lifesciences develops new revenue drivers to sustain growth
articles.summary: Zydus Lifesciences is focusing on rare disease treatments and vaccines to offset falling revenue from three major products in the US. The company is developing a portfolio including generic Semaglutide and Saroglitazar. Despite short-term earnings stalls, long-term growth is expected through recent acquisitions in consumer health and medtech.

articles.headline: Bosch faces tempered growth expectations amid sector cyclicality
articles.summary: Auto component supplier Bosch is expected to see a production growth rate of 6-7% in FY27, down from 11% in FY26. While the company maintains strong financials and is expanding into EV components through a joint venture with Tata AutoComp, current high valuations and global commodity price pressures suggest a cautious 'hold' strategy for investors.

articles.headline: F&O Strategy: Technical bounce expected for Dixon Technologies
articles.summary: Dixon Technologies is currently at a crucial support level after a sharp slide. Market experts expect a technical bounce, recommending a long position on April futures with a stop-loss at ₹9,732. The contract has seen fresh short build-up, and a close above ₹13,350 would be required to turn the long-term outlook positive.

articles.headline: Stock exchanges announce revisions to derivative lot sizes
articles.summary: The NSE and BSE have announced revisions to the lot sizes of derivative contracts to keep contract values within the ₹5-10 lakh range. The lot size of 43 stocks will be increased, while five stocks will see a reduction. Revisions will take effect from the May series on the BSE and the July series on the NSE.

articles.headline: Equity indices test critical support levels for future trend
articles.summary: Benchmark indices Nifty 50 and Sensex have fallen for six consecutive weeks, testing crucial supports at 22,200 and 71,500 respectively. Analysts state that price action in April will be vital in determining the market trend for the rest of the year. FPIs have sold approximately $14.8 billion in Indian equities over the last five weeks.

articles.headline: Effective strategies for fixed income and bond investing
articles.summary: With risk assets under pressure, bond strategies such as accrual, duration, laddering, and barbell investing are gaining traction. Accrual suits conservative investors, while laddering helps manage interest rate risk by spreading maturities. Target maturity funds (TMFs) are also highlighted as a simple way for retail investors to lock in predictable returns.

articles.headline: Indian pharma exports grow 5.6% to $28 billion
articles.summary: India’s pharmaceutical exports reached $28.29 billion during the April-February FY26 period. Pharmexcil DG K Raja Bhanu stated that the industry is valued at $60 billion and is on track to hit $130 billion by 2030. Officials urged the industry to reduce import dependence on raw materials to insulate exports from global geopolitical uncertainties.

articles.headline: Switch Mobility CEO targets 10,000 electric vehicles in two years
articles.summary: Switch Mobility, the EV arm of Ashok Leyland, plans to double its fleet to 10,000 vehicles over the next two years. CEO Ganesh Mani highlighted global expansion plans anchored by a new manufacturing plant in the UAE. The company achieved EBITDA breakeven in FY26 and is now focusing on a self-sustained operating model despite rising battery and commodity costs.

articles.headline: Rajasthan Plans Investors’ Outreach Via Roadshows
articles.summary: Rajasthan government will roll out roadshows in several cities to attract investments in the agriculture sector ahead of the Global Rajasthan Agritech Meet.

articles.headline: Looking for Ideal Investment Advice During Times of War
articles.summary: Experts caution that 'buy the dip' strategies may not work in one-time events like war, advising caution and awareness of investor incentives.

articles.headline: FY26 Pharma Export Likely By Mid-April: Rajesh Agrawal
articles.summary: Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal expects FY26 pharma export data by mid-April, anticipating positive performance despite global challenges.

articles.headline: RBI Set for Anti-Mis-selling Norms, Focus on Incentives
articles.summary: The RBI is expected to announce new guidelines on responsible business conduct to prevent mis-selling by banks, focusing on transparent insurance distribution.

articles.headline: Weighty Problem: Input Costs for Plastic Sector Still High
articles.summary: Input costs for the plastic sector remain high despite Customs Duty exemptions, impacting prices of plastic products due to increased raw material costs.

articles.headline: Jagiroad’s Poll Story: Semiconductor Plant Offers Hope
articles.summary: The upcoming semiconductor plant in Jagiroad is seen as a game-changer for Assam's economy, offering employment and development opportunities.

articles.headline: Tourism sector shows signs of strong revival post-pandemic
articles.summary: The tourism sector is showing promising signs of revival following the pandemic, with increased domestic and international travel. New initiatives are being launched to attract more tourists.

articles.headline: New initiatives to boost domestic manufacturing capacity
articles.summary: The Ministry of Commerce announced new initiatives today designed to enhance domestic manufacturing capabilities and reduce reliance on imports, fostering self-sufficiency.

articles.headline: New report highlights changing consumer trends in urban areas
articles.summary: A recent market research report indicates significant shifts in consumer behavior and purchasing patterns in urban areas, influenced by e-commerce and changing lifestyle preferences.

articles.headline: New international trade agreements signed, boosting bilateral commerce
articles.summary: Several new bilateral trade agreements were signed today between key nations, expected to significantly increase commerce and investment flows between them.

articles.headline: Small businesses report positive growth trends in local market
articles.summary: Local small businesses are experiencing positive growth, supported by community initiatives and increasing consumer demand, contributing to the local economy.

articles.headline: EC Suspends Four Cops Over Law and Order Failure in West Bengal
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India suspended four police officials, including a Deputy Commissioner, in West Bengal over alleged law and order lapses during a roadshow attended by Union Home Minister Amit Shah.

articles.headline: Patna's Bootlegging Boom: Prohibition Fuels Crime
articles.summary: Bihar's strict prohibition laws have fuelled a bootlegging industry, with rising prices for illicit alcohol and concerns about public health due to potential poisonings.

articles.headline: EC Roll Deletions Heartbreaking for Dedicated Democrats
articles.summary: Voters, particularly in West Bengal, are expressing frustration and heartbreak over being removed from electoral rolls, impacting their ability to participate in upcoming elections.

articles.headline: BJP white paper highlights financial crisis in Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu BJP has released a white paper claiming the state is in a 'financial crisis' under the DMK government. Union Minister Piyush Goyal stated that the state's revenue deficit reached ₹69,129 crore and outstanding debt is projected to hit ₹10.71 lakh crore by March 2027. He accused the state government of failing to account for ₹80,000 crore in GST collections.

articles.headline: PMK president flags Tamil Nadu debt surge under DMK
articles.summary: PMK President Anbumani Ramadoss raised concerns over Tamil Nadu's rising liabilities, which he claims have doubled to ₹10 lakh crore in the last five years. He criticized the current administration for using borrowings to meet routine expenses rather than asset creation. He called for a change in governance to ensure accountability and fiscal health.

articles.headline: Instagram Rewrites Visibility for Politics, Rewards Outrage
articles.summary: Instagram is emerging as India's new political battleground, favouring outrage and potentially muting dissent. This shift is reshaping how political visibility is achieved and perceived.

articles.headline: PM: All States Will Benefit from Proposed Delimitation
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi assures that all states will benefit from the proposed delimitation, aiming to address concerns over the Lok Sabha seat allocation exercise.

articles.headline: T.N. CM, Union Minister Clash Over Three-Language Formula
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu CM Stalin criticized the CBSE's three-language policy as 'Hindi imposition,' while Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan called it a 'tired attempt to mask political failures.'

articles.headline: AAP Accuses Chadha of Ignoring Punjab’s Issues; Allegations Scripted: MP
articles.summary: AAP accused Raghav Chadha of failing to raise Punjab's issues in Parliament. Chadha denied the claims, calling them 'false' and part of a 'coordinated campaign.'

articles.headline: Stalin Challenges Modi to Tell T.N. Voters That ‘Hindi Will Be Imposed’
articles.summary: M.K. Stalin dared PM Modi to declare Hindi imposition in Tamil Nadu, calling the FCRA Amendment Bill an attack on religious freedom.

articles.headline: UDF Fighting Combined Force of LDF, BJP in Kerala: Rahul
articles.summary: Rahul Gandhi stated the UDF is fighting a combined force of LDF and BJP in Kerala, accusing the CM of aligning with right-wing forces.

articles.headline: Congress Moves EC Against Kairali TV Over ‘Defamation’
articles.summary: The Congress has filed a complaint with the Election Commission against Kairali TV for allegedly disseminating fabricated photos to defame AICC general secretary K.C. Venugopal.

articles.headline: Bengal CM Dares Shah to Speak to People Deleted from Rolls
articles.summary: Mamata Banerjee challenged Amit Shah to meet people whose names were deleted from electoral rolls in Malda, alleging BJP stripped citizens of voting rights.

articles.headline: Ro Khanna: Exploring the New Middle
articles.summary: Rep. Ro Khanna is forging a new political path by combining nationalism and welfarism, challenging establishment norms and exploring presidential ambitions.

articles.headline: Assam CM and Cong. Continue Poll War of Words
articles.summary: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma and Congress leaders are engaged in a heated exchange of words over corruption and governance issues ahead of the polls.

articles.headline: Cong.-BJP Nexus Claim Absurd, Says Chennithala
articles.summary: Senior Congress leader Ramesh Chennithala dismissed allegations of a nexus between Congress and BJP in Kerala, affirming commitment to addressing public needs.

articles.headline: Key political leaders meet to discuss upcoming national elections
articles.summary: Top political figures from ruling and opposition parties held crucial talks today to strategize for the forthcoming national elections. Discussions reportedly covered alliance formations and campaign agendas, signaling a potential shift in the political landscape.

articles.headline: Heated debates continue in state assembly over education bill
articles.summary: The state legislative assembly witnessed intense debates today regarding a controversial new education reform bill. Opposition parties raised concerns about implementation challenges and funding, while the ruling party defended the bill's objectives.

articles.headline: Opposition party plans nationwide protests against new tax laws
articles.summary: A major opposition party has announced plans for nationwide protests against the government's recently implemented tax laws, claiming they are detrimental to small businesses and common citizens.

articles.headline: Government announces reforms to improve public service delivery
articles.summary: The government today unveiled a series of reforms aimed at streamlining processes and enhancing efficiency in public service delivery, ensuring better access and quality for citizens.

articles.headline: Political analysts forecast significant shifts in upcoming elections
articles.summary: Political analysts are predicting a potentially significant realignment of the political landscape in the lead-up to the next general elections, citing changing voter demographics and regional alliances.

articles.headline: Local municipal body holds crucial meeting on urban services
articles.summary: The local municipal corporation held a key meeting today to discuss improving urban services, including waste management, public transport, and civic infrastructure upgrades.

articles.headline: Time to Unlock the Silver Abundance
articles.summary: India's demographic shift towards an older population presents an opportunity to reimagine systems, narratives, and institutions, focusing on the 'silver economy' beyond just healthcare.

articles.headline: Govt to increase gas supply to fertilizer units and industries
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas will increase natural gas supplies to fertilizer plants and industrial consumers to 90% of their average consumption starting April 6. The move aims to stabilize production amid global supply disruptions linked to West Asian tensions. Piped natural gas (PNG) and CNG for transport will continue to receive prioritized 100% supply.

articles.headline: India resumes energy trade with Iran after seven years
articles.summary: Indian refiners have sourced crude oil and LPG from Iran for the first time since May 2019. This follows the US lifting sanctions on Iranian crude as West Asian conflicts prolong the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, choking 20% of global supplies. India is utilizing a rupee payment mechanism through UCO Bank to facilitate these transactions and secure its energy requirements.

articles.headline: S&P expert sees no systemic risk in US private credit
articles.summary: Ramki Muthukrishnan of S&P Global Ratings states that the $2.5-trillion US private credit market is not heading for a 2008-style crisis. Despite a trailing 12-month default rate of 4.45%, the industry remains well-collateralized. While AI disruption poses risks to software-linked loans, most borrowers have demonstrated stable revenue and cash flows.

articles.headline: OMCs to receive ₹17,500 crore for LPG under-recoveries
articles.summary: The government will release ₹17,500 crore in seven tranches to IOCL, BPCL, and HPCL during FY27. This is the remaining portion of a ₹30,000 crore compensation package approved in August 2025 to cover losses on domestic LPG. Under-recoveries were driven by high international prices and reached roughly ₹220 per 14.2 kg cylinder in the last fiscal year.

articles.headline: Plastic manufacturers in Tamil Nadu face 50% production cut
articles.summary: MSME plastic units in Tamil Nadu and Pondicherry report a 50% cut in production as polymer prices surged 70% in March due to the West Asia war. The industry body TANPPA has requested the Central government to exempt 18% GST on recycled plastic and implement price controls similar to those used for fuel to prevent artificial scarcity.

articles.headline: Government unveils major policy reform package for economic growth
articles.summary: The government today unveiled a comprehensive package aimed at boosting economic growth, focusing on industrial incentives and investment. Experts anticipate significant impact on employment and GDP. Details of the fiscal measures and sector-specific policies were outlined in a press briefing.

articles.headline: Government reviews impact of recent economic policies
articles.summary: A high-level committee today commenced a comprehensive review of the government's recent economic policies, assessing their impact on various sectors and population segments. Preliminary findings are expected next month.

articles.headline: Foreign direct investment surges, boosting economic confidence
articles.summary: India witnessed a significant surge in foreign direct investment (FDI) in the last quarter, reflecting growing international investor confidence. This influx is expected to create new jobs and stimulate manufacturing.

articles.headline: Government considers further economic stimulus measures
articles.summary: Following a recent downturn, the government is reportedly contemplating additional economic stimulus measures to inject liquidity into the market and support struggling industries.

articles.headline: Indian banking sector reports robust performance in Q3
articles.summary: The Indian banking sector has reported strong financial performance in the third quarter, driven by increased credit growth and improved asset quality. Analysts anticipate continued stability.

articles.headline: IMF issues updated global economic forecast, warns of challenges
articles.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) today released an updated global economic forecast, projecting moderate growth but cautioning about potential headwinds from inflation and geopolitical tensions.

articles.headline: In-depth analysis reveals nuances of recent economic policy impact
articles.summary: An expert analysis offers a nuanced view of the impact of recent economic policies, highlighting both intended benefits and unforeseen challenges across different sectors of the economy.

articles.headline: Perumbavoor Migrant Tensions Expose Political Blind Spot
articles.summary: Tensions between migrant labourers and locals in Perumbavoor, Kerala, over crime and integration highlight a political blind spot in election campaigns, despite the town's reliance on migrant labour.

articles.headline: Majuli Electors Welcome Connectivity, Fear Influx of Outsiders
articles.summary: New bridges are improving connectivity to Majuli island, Assam, but residents welcome the development while also fearing an influx of outsiders and potential dilution of their culture.

articles.headline: Illegal Billing, Manipulation of Receipts Rampant at MCD Parking Lots
articles.summary: Spot checks revealed illegal overcharging and manipulated receipts at several MCD parking lots in Delhi, with officials planning system revamps.

articles.headline: No Cooking Gas Shortage, Don't Panic: CM to Citizens
articles.summary: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta urged citizens not to panic about LPG, stating the administration is ensuring an uninterrupted supply and strict action against violations.

articles.headline: LeT 'Handler' Shabir Ahmed Lone Sent to 7-Day Custody
articles.summary: A Delhi court sent Lashkar-e-Taiba handler Shabir Ahmed Lone to seven days of judicial custody after his arrest on March 29.

articles.headline: Faceless Suppliers, Digital Transactions Pose Tougher Challenges to Police
articles.summary: The rise of social media and digital payments complicates drug trafficking investigations, pushing Delhi police to enhance technical capabilities.

articles.headline: Chester Hill Case: Sukhu Govt. Directs Fresh Probe
articles.summary: Himachal Pradesh government ordered a fresh probe into the Chester Hill land case over allegations of benami land deal and violation of land laws.

articles.headline: Border tensions remain high, military deploys additional forces
articles.summary: Escalating tensions along the northern border prompted the military to deploy additional troops and surveillance assets. Officials are closely monitoring the situation, while diplomatic channels remain open for de-escalation efforts. No major incidents reported recently.

articles.headline: New rural development program launched to uplift villages
articles.summary: A new government program focusing on rural development was inaugurated today, aiming to provide essential services like clean water, sanitation, and skill training to remote villages across the state.

articles.headline: Government initiates dialogue on proposed agricultural reforms
articles.summary: The government has begun consultations with farmer unions and agricultural experts to discuss new proposed reforms aimed at modernizing the agriculture sector and increasing farmer incomes.

articles.headline: Animal welfare organizations call for stronger protection laws
articles.summary: Animal welfare organizations are advocating for stricter laws and greater public awareness to prevent cruelty and ensure better protection for animals across the region.

articles.headline: New community development fund established for local projects
articles.summary: A new community development fund has been set up to support local projects, including infrastructure improvements, educational initiatives, and healthcare services in underserved areas.

articles.headline: Iran Attacks Israel-Linked Vessel in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Iran's drones attacked an Israel-affiliated vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, causing a fire, amidst ongoing hunts for missing US pilots after two warplanes were downed.

articles.headline: Ukraine Claims Hit on Russian Nitrogen Plant
articles.summary: Ukraine announced it struck a nitrogen fertilizer plant in Russia's Tolyatti overnight, while Russian forces targeted energy facilities in Ukraine, escalating conflict.

articles.headline: Global money managers moderate exposure as West Asia war persists
articles.summary: Global investment firms are scaling back growth-stock exposure in favor of value stocks as the conflict in Iran keeps oil prices above $100. Markets have been volatile following US President Trump’s recent address, which offered no framework for reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Analysts warned that prolonged high energy costs could exacerbate a K-shaped economic recovery.

articles.headline: Trump Gives Iran 48 Hours to Make Deal or Face 'Hell'
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump issued an ultimatum to Iran, giving them 48 hours to make a deal or face severe consequences amid rising tensions.

articles.headline: India Has Solidly Dealt with Recent Global Challenges: Jaishankar
articles.summary: EAM S. Jaishankar stated India has navigated recent global challenges like the pandemic and conflicts effectively, emerging stronger.

articles.headline: Israel Strikes South Lebanon’s Tyre After Issuing Evacuation Warnings
articles.summary: Israel renewed strikes on Tyre, Lebanon, after evacuation warnings, damaging buildings including a hospital and a bridge.

articles.headline: Mohammad Ishaq Dar: Pakistan's Diplomatic Reboot
articles.summary: Foreign Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar is leading Pakistan's diplomatic efforts, restarting ties with Bangladesh and mediating in the Gulf conflict.

articles.headline: Leaders attend major international summit on global cooperation
articles.summary: Heads of state and government convened at a major international summit today to address pressing global challenges, including climate change, economic stability, and regional security.

articles.headline: Diplomatic efforts intensify to resolve ongoing regional conflict
articles.summary: Diplomatic efforts have been intensified by international mediators to broker a peaceful resolution to an enduring regional conflict, with hopes for a ceasefire agreement.

articles.headline: International organizations step up humanitarian aid efforts
articles.summary: Major international humanitarian organizations are scaling up their aid operations in crisis-affected regions, providing essential supplies, food, and medical assistance to vulnerable populations.

articles.headline: Raja Ravi Varma's 'Yashoda and Krishna' Sets New Auction Record
articles.summary: Raja Ravi Varma's 'Yashoda and Krishna' sold for `167.20 crore, setting a new auction record for the artist and highlighting a maturing Indian art market.

articles.headline: Hong Kong's Haute Tables: Four Exciting New Culinary Options
articles.summary: Hong Kong's vibrant food scene features new culinary hotspots like Torikaze (yakitori), Roucou (French-Japanese fusion), Moolah (fusion), and Right Tea-Rific (Cantonese classics).

articles.headline: D'Artagnan's Skeleton Possibly Found in Dutch Church
articles.summary: A skeleton possibly belonging to d'Artagnan, the inspiration for 'The Three Musketeers', has been discovered in Maastricht, Netherlands, centuries after his death.

articles.headline: A Hearth of Sufism: Zool Festival Keeps Legacy Alive
articles.summary: The annual Zool festival in Aishmuqam, Kashmir, commemorates Sufi saint Baba Zain-ud-Din Wali, symbolising light over darkness and heralding spring.

articles.headline: Preparations in full swing for annual state cultural festival
articles.summary: Preparations are well underway for the state's annual cultural festival, featuring traditional music, dance, and art forms. The event aims to promote local heritage and attract tourism.

articles.headline: Renowned artist's new exhibition opens to critical acclaim
articles.summary: A highly anticipated art exhibition by a renowned contemporary artist opened today, showcasing a collection of new works that explore themes of identity and social commentary.

articles.headline: Efforts intensify to preserve ancient cultural heritage sites
articles.summary: Conservation efforts are being intensified to protect and restore several ancient cultural heritage sites facing decay, with new funding allocated for restoration projects.

articles.headline: Major restoration work begins on historic city landmark
articles.summary: Extensive restoration work has commenced on a prominent historical landmark in the city, aiming to preserve its structural integrity and historical significance for future generations.

articles.headline: Annual performing arts festival showcases diverse regional talent
articles.summary: The annual performing arts festival is currently ongoing, attracting audiences with a diverse array of traditional and contemporary performances from various regions.

articles.headline: Government supports initiatives to revive traditional heritage crafts
articles.summary: Initiatives are being rolled out to support local artisans and revive traditional heritage crafts, ensuring the continuity of valuable cultural practices and providing livelihoods.

articles.headline: Annual local artisans fair attracts large crowds and buyers
articles.summary: The annual local artisans fair, showcasing handmade crafts and traditional products, has attracted a large number of visitors and buyers, supporting local livelihoods.

articles.headline: Popular heritage walk program extended due to public demand
articles.summary: A popular heritage walk program, exploring the historical sites of the city, has been extended due to overwhelming public demand, offering more tours for enthusiasts.

articles.headline: Popular art workshop series launched for young aspiring artists
articles.summary: A series of art workshops, focusing on various techniques and styles, has been launched for young aspiring artists, aiming to foster creativity and artistic skills.

articles.headline: Vibrant local music festival showcases diverse musical traditions
articles.summary: The annual local music festival brought together artists and enthusiasts, celebrating diverse musical traditions and contemporary genres, attracting a large audience.

articles.headline: Haaland's Hat-trick Destroys Liverpool; Man City into FA Cup Semis
articles.summary: Erling Haaland scored a hat-trick as Manchester City thrashed Liverpool 4-0 to reach the FA Cup semi-finals, intensifying pressure on Liverpool's manager Arne Slot.

articles.headline: Whose World Cup Is It? Exclusivity in Global Game
articles.summary: Rising visa costs, soaring ticket prices, and qualification misses for major teams like Italy and Iran are making the 'global' game feel increasingly exclusive.

articles.headline: Vishvanath Stuns World Champion to Reach Asian Boxing Semis
articles.summary: India's Vishvanath Suresh upset reigning world champion Sanzhar Tashkenbay of Kazakhstan to reach the men's 50kg semifinals of the Asian Boxing Championships.

articles.headline: Sreeshankar Wins Long Jump Title with 8.15m Leap
articles.summary: Murali Sreeshankar won the men's long jump title at the Indian Athletics Series with an 8.15m leap, marking a strong comeback after knee surgery.

articles.headline: Royals Pip Titans in Thriller; Rizvi Powers Capitals to Victory
articles.summary: Rajasthan Royals narrowly defeated Gujarat Titans in a thrilling match, while Riyan Parag's performance powered Delhi Capitals to victory against Mumbai Indians.

articles.headline: Payal Stuns Sheetal to Bag Compound Women’s Title
articles.summary: Para-archer Payal Nag defeated World Champion Sheetal Devi to win the compound women's title at the World Para Series in Bangkok.

articles.headline: 'Super Sub' Ritwik Helps Jamshedpur Snatch Draw Against Mohun Bagan
articles.summary: Ritwik Das scored in stoppage time as Jamshedpur FC snatched a 1-1 draw against Mohun Bagan Super Giant in the ISL.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Consigns Goa to Its First Defeat of the Season
articles.summary: Bengaluru FC defeated FC Goa 2-0, handing Goa its first defeat of the ISL season and securing a memorable win for Bengaluru.

articles.headline: Can Super Giants Score Over Sunrisers?
articles.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad's strong batting faces Lucknow Super Giants' improved bowling in a crucial IPL clash, with both teams aiming for momentum.

articles.headline: Advani and Chitra Bag Memorable Doubles Title
articles.summary: Pankaj Advani and Chitra Magimairaj won memorable doubles titles at the Pool Nationals, showcasing strong performances.

articles.headline: Sreeshankar and Ancy Jump to Gold and Glory
articles.summary: Murali Sreeshankar and Ancy Sojan secured gold medals in the men's and women's long jump events at the Indian Athletics Series-1.

articles.headline: ‘Arun Effect’ on AFI’s New Directive
articles.summary: AFI's new directive requiring athletes to get approval before signing sponsorship deals aims to ensure fair agreements and prevent exploitation by agents.

articles.headline: Delhi and Blasters Eye First Victory in Clash of Laggards
articles.summary: Sporting Club Delhi and Kerala Blasters, both struggling, aim for their first ISL victory when they clash on Sunday.

articles.headline: Bayern Roars Back with Late Goals to Sink Freiburg
articles.summary: Bayern Munich scored three goals in the final nine minutes to come from behind and defeat Freiburg 3-2, maintaining their Bundesliga lead.

articles.headline: National cricket team announces squad for upcoming international series
articles.summary: The national cricket board today unveiled the squad for the highly anticipated international series, featuring a mix of experienced players and promising young talent.

articles.headline: New state-of-the-art athlete training facility inaugurated
articles.summary: A cutting-edge sports training facility, equipped with advanced technology and expert coaches, was inaugurated today, aiming to nurture future Olympic medalists.

articles.headline: Government launches initiatives to promote youth participation in sports
articles.summary: The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports has announced several new initiatives aimed at encouraging greater participation of children and youth in various sports across the country.

articles.headline: National football league season kicks off with thrilling matches
articles.summary: The new season of the national football league commenced today, witnessing several thrilling matches and unexpected upsets. Fans are excited for a competitive season ahead.

articles.headline: Indian athletes gear up for Olympic qualifiers with intensive training
articles.summary: Elite Indian athletes are undergoing rigorous training camps as they prepare for the upcoming Olympic qualifying events, aiming to secure berths and represent the nation.

articles.headline: Local tennis star wins prestigious national tournament title
articles.summary: A rising local tennis star clinched the title at the prestigious national tournament, displaying exceptional talent and determination throughout the competitive event.

articles.headline: International badminton championship draws top global players
articles.summary: An international badminton championship currently underway is attracting top-ranked players from around the globe, competing for significant prize money and ranking points.

articles.headline: Athletics Federation discusses roadmap for grassroots development
articles.summary: The national Athletics Federation held a crucial meeting to outline a comprehensive roadmap for grassroots development programs, focusing on identifying and nurturing young talent.

articles.headline: Annual local sports event concludes with high participation
articles.summary: The annual local sports event concluded successfully, witnessing high participation from various age groups and promoting community engagement through athletic competitions.

articles.headline: NHB launches Gruh Sugam portal for government personnel
articles.summary: The National Housing Bank has launched the Gruh Sugam Portal to enable defense and government personnel to avail home loans digitally from their posting locations. The initiative aims to accelerate digital lending adoption and financial inclusion, allowing members of paramilitary and government services to process loan approvals without visiting physical bank branches.

articles.headline: New infrastructure project inaugurated in western region
articles.summary: A significant new infrastructure project, including a major highway expansion and a new bridge, was inaugurated today in the western region. The project aims to improve connectivity and facilitate trade, benefiting local communities and businesses.

articles.headline: City administration announces new urban planning initiatives
articles.summary: The city administration today unveiled several new urban planning initiatives aimed at decongesting traffic, improving public transport, and creating green spaces. Public feedback is invited for the proposed master plan.

articles.headline: Review finds key infrastructure projects on track for completion
articles.summary: A recent government review confirms that major infrastructure development projects, including highways and ports, are progressing on schedule and are expected to meet their completion deadlines.

articles.headline: City water supply system undergoes major upgrades
articles.summary: The city's aging water supply infrastructure is undergoing significant upgrades to ensure uninterrupted and clean drinking water for all residents, with project completion expected next year.

articles.headline: New traffic management plan implemented to ease city congestion
articles.summary: A revised traffic management plan has been implemented in the city center, introducing new routes and signaling systems to alleviate severe traffic congestion during peak hours.

articles.headline: New road safety campaign launched to reduce accidents
articles.summary: A new road safety campaign is underway, using public awareness programs and stricter enforcement to reduce the number of traffic accidents and promote safe driving habits.

articles.headline: Government announces new projects for rural infrastructure development
articles.summary: The government has sanctioned several new projects aimed at enhancing rural infrastructure, including better roads, electrification, and improved communication networks in remote areas.

articles.headline: CRED introduces biometric authentication for UPI payments
articles.summary: Fintech platform CRED has rolled out biometric authentication for UPI transactions up to ₹5,000. Members can now authorize payments using face recognition or fingerprints instead of a PIN on both iOS and Android devices. The feature went live on March 25 and applies to all UPI use cases on the platform.

articles.headline: Digital economy projected to reach 20% of GDP by 2030
articles.summary: MeitY Secretary S Krishnan stated that India's digital economy is expected to grow from 12% in 2022 to 20% of total GSDP by 2030. Speaking at the inauguration of SASTRA’s new electronics and semiconductor facilities, he emphasized that physical infrastructure and skilling are critical for India to become the world's third-largest economy.

articles.headline: 'Cloning' Hurdle Skirted to Perfectly Copy Quantum State
articles.summary: Physicists have demonstrated a loophole in the no-cloning theorem, allowing for perfect copies of quantum states if encrypted, potentially enabling quantum cloud storage.

articles.headline: Technology start-up ecosystem continues rapid growth across cities
articles.summary: India's technology start-up ecosystem is experiencing robust growth, with new ventures emerging in various cities. Government initiatives and private funding are fueling this expansion, fostering innovation.

articles.headline: Government accelerates digital transformation projects across ministries
articles.summary: Efforts to digitalize government services are being accelerated, with several new projects launched to streamline administrative processes and improve public service delivery through technology.

articles.headline: New cybersecurity measures implemented to protect government data
articles.summary: The government has rolled out advanced cybersecurity measures to safeguard sensitive digital data and critical infrastructure from increasing cyber threats and attacks.

articles.headline: Experts debate ethical implications of rapid AI development
articles.summary: Leading artificial intelligence experts convened to discuss the ethical considerations surrounding the rapid advancements in AI technology, emphasizing responsible development and deployment.

articles.headline: Nationwide digital literacy campaign launched in rural areas
articles.summary: A new nationwide campaign aims to boost digital literacy in rural areas, providing training and access to technology to bridge the digital divide and empower communities.

articles.headline: More digital governance initiatives rolled out for citizen convenience
articles.summary: Further digital governance initiatives are being introduced to enhance citizen convenience, offering online access to various government services and reducing bureaucratic hurdles.

articles.headline: Plans announced for establishing new technology innovation hubs
articles.summary: New technology innovation hubs are planned for establishment in major cities, designed to facilitate collaboration between academia, industry, and startups, driving technological advancement.

articles.headline: Digital payment methods see increased adoption in small towns
articles.summary: The use of digital payment platforms is rapidly increasing in smaller towns and rural areas, driven by ease of access and government initiatives promoting cashless transactions.

articles.headline: New start-up incubation center opens to support innovators
articles.summary: A new incubation center has opened its doors, providing mentorship, funding, and resources to promising technology startups, fostering innovation and entrepreneurship.

articles.headline: Artemis II Takes Humans Around Moon: A New Space Race
articles.summary: The Artemis II mission aims to send humans around the moon, signaling a new era in the space race, with technological advancements driving exploration.

articles.headline: Nuclear Fusion Cost Models Too Optimistic to be Viable: Experts
articles.summary: Researchers suggest that nuclear fusion cost projections are overly optimistic, potentially misallocating funds needed for other climate mitigation efforts.

articles.headline: New Data Explain How Black Holes Have ‘Forbidden Masses’
articles.summary: Scientists have explained the existence of black holes in 'forbidden' mass ranges through hierarchical mergers, where lighter black holes merge to form heavier ones.

articles.headline: Speeding Up Enzyme Reactions in Microbes Using Light
articles.summary: Researchers are using visible light to trigger inherent enzymes in microbes for producing useful products and drugs, a technique called photobiocatalysis.

articles.headline: Mission Moon: US vs. China's Different Approaches
articles.summary: The US frames its moon missions as a geopolitical race, while China views them as part of a science-driven national development program.

articles.headline: Indian scientists achieve breakthrough in chronic disease research
articles.summary: A team of Indian scientists has announced a significant breakthrough in research for a chronic disease, potentially paving the way for new diagnostic tools and therapeutic interventions.

articles.headline: India's latest space mission achieves key orbital maneuver
articles.summary: India's ambitious new space mission successfully performed a critical orbital maneuver today, bringing it closer to its primary scientific objectives. Scientists express optimism about data collection.

articles.headline: Scientists announce breakthrough in renewable energy storage technology
articles.summary: A team of researchers has developed a groundbreaking new technology for more efficient and cost-effective renewable energy storage, potentially revolutionizing the energy sector.

articles.headline: Global scientific collaboration tackles emerging health threats
articles.summary: International scientific collaboration is intensifying to research and combat emerging global health threats, sharing data and expertise to develop rapid responses and new treatments.

articles.headline: Increased government funding allocated for scientific research
articles.summary: The government has announced a substantial increase in funding for scientific research and development, aiming to foster innovation and bolster the nation's scientific capabilities.

articles.headline: Centre Can’t Treat States as ‘Subordinates’, Says Justice Nagarathna
articles.summary: Supreme Court Justice B.V. Nagarathna stated the Centre cannot treat states as subordinates and should not discriminate against citizens based on political differences.

articles.headline: Threat to Self-Identification: Trans Activists Move SC
articles.summary: Transgender activists have moved the Supreme Court challenging the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act, 2026, citing concerns over self-identification rights.

articles.headline: Rajasthan High Court Upholds Cancellation of 2021 SI Recruitment Exam
articles.summary: Rajasthan High Court upheld the cancellation of the 2021 SI recruitment exam due to irregularities and paper leaks, directing RPSC to ensure transparency.

articles.headline: Police investigate recent increase in petty crime incidents
articles.summary: Local police are investigating a recent surge in petty crime incidents reported in residential areas. Patrols have been increased, and community members are urged to report suspicious activities.

articles.headline: New judicial appointments announced for High Court vacancies
articles.summary: Several new appointments to the High Court have been announced to fill long-standing vacancies, aiming to expedite judicial processes and reduce case backlogs.

articles.headline: Progress reported in implementation of judicial reforms package
articles.summary: Significant progress has been reported in the implementation of a comprehensive judicial reforms package, with measures to reduce case pendency and enhance legal accessibility.

articles.headline: New community policing program launched to enhance safety
articles.summary: A new community policing program has been initiated in several neighborhoods, aiming to build trust between police and residents and deter crime through collaborative efforts.

articles.headline: Key local court verdicts delivered in property dispute cases
articles.summary: The local court today delivered important verdicts in several long-standing property dispute cases, providing clarity and resolution for affected parties.

articles.headline: Legal aid program expands to assist vulnerable communities
articles.summary: A free legal aid program has expanded its services to reach more vulnerable communities, providing essential legal counsel and assistance to those who cannot afford it.

articles.headline: City Doctors Welcome Newborn Screening Under ANMOL Scheme
articles.summary: Doctors in Delhi welcome the expanded newborn screening under the ANMOL scheme, which will enable early diagnosis of various conditions.

articles.headline: Devotees Fall Ill After Having ‘Badam Milk’ at Village Festival
articles.summary: Several devotees fell ill after consuming ‘badam milk’ at a village festival in Andhra Pradesh, prompting a swift response from authorities.

articles.headline: Public health advisory issued for monsoon season diseases
articles.summary: Health authorities issued a public advisory today, cautioning citizens about prevalent diseases during the ongoing monsoon season. Recommendations include hygiene practices and immediate medical consultation for symptoms. Campaigns are planned for public awareness.

articles.headline: Ambitious plans unveiled for nationwide healthcare infrastructure expansion
articles.summary: The Health Ministry has revealed ambitious plans for expanding healthcare infrastructure across the country, including new hospitals, clinics, and increased medical staff training.

articles.headline: Report highlights rising fitness and wellness trends among urbanites
articles.summary: A new report indicates a growing interest in fitness and wellness activities among urban populations, leading to an increase in health clubs and specialized training programs.

articles.headline: Promising new medical treatment shows positive results in trials
articles.summary: Early clinical trials for a novel medical treatment targeting a severe illness have yielded promising positive results, offering hope for patients worldwide. Further trials are planned.

articles.headline: Focus on mental health awareness programs for adolescents
articles.summary: Health organizations are increasing efforts to promote mental health awareness and support programs specifically tailored for adolescents, addressing growing concerns.

articles.headline: Government updates national healthcare policies for broader coverage
articles.summary: National healthcare policies have been updated to ensure broader coverage and better access to medical services for all citizens, including new provisions for vulnerable groups.

articles.headline: India strengthens global health preparedness in partnership with WHO
articles.summary: India is enhancing its capabilities for global health preparedness, collaborating with the World Health Organization (WHO) on disease surveillance and rapid response mechanisms.

articles.headline: Free healthcare camp benefits thousands in rural district
articles.summary: A free healthcare camp organized in a rural district successfully provided medical consultations, diagnostic tests, and medicines to thousands of underprivileged residents.

articles.headline: Free community health check-up camps organized for residents
articles.summary: Several free community health check-up camps are being organized in various localities, providing essential medical screenings and consultations to residents.

articles.headline: New public health campaign focuses on preventative care
articles.summary: A new public health campaign has been launched, emphasizing the importance of preventative care, regular check-ups, and healthy lifestyle choices to improve overall community health.

articles.headline: U.P. CM Pushes for Achieving Zero Dropout Rate in Schools
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath appealed for zero dropout rate in schools, highlighting the state's progress in improving school enrollment.

articles.headline: ‘Chinese Researchers Account for Too Many Retractions’
articles.summary: An analysis found authors affiliated with China accounted for over 52% of retractions, attributed to 'publish or perish' cultures and paper mills.

articles.headline: Major reforms proposed for higher education system
articles.summary: A new policy document outlines significant reforms for the higher education system, aiming to enhance quality, promote research, and improve employability of graduates.

articles.headline: Government expands skill development programs for youth
articles.summary: New skill development programs have been launched nationwide, targeting unemployed youth with training in emerging technologies and vocational trades to enhance employability.

articles.headline: New university admission policy aims for greater inclusivity
articles.summary: Universities have announced a revised admission policy designed to foster greater inclusivity and access for students from diverse backgrounds, with special provisions for underprivileged sections.

articles.headline: Adult literacy drive reports significant progress in eastern states
articles.summary: The national adult literacy drive has achieved significant progress in the eastern states, with thousands of adults gaining foundational reading and writing skills.

articles.headline: Growth of online learning platforms continues post-pandemic
articles.summary: The adoption of online learning platforms continues to grow, providing flexible educational opportunities for students and professionals, especially in remote areas.

articles.headline: Increased school enrollment reported after public awareness drive
articles.summary: A recent public awareness campaign has led to an increase in school enrollments, particularly in primary education, showcasing success in promoting access to education.

articles.headline: City's public libraries undergo modernization and expansion
articles.summary: The city's public library network is undergoing a major modernization and expansion project, adding digital resources and new community learning spaces for residents.

articles.headline: Vocational training programs see record enrollment numbers
articles.summary: Government-sponsored vocational training programs have reported record enrollment figures, indicating a growing interest among youth in acquiring job-oriented skills.

articles.headline: PETA Urges Russian Artist to Withdraw ‘Pink Elephant’ Print
articles.summary: PETA India urged Russian photographer Julia Buruleva to withdraw a commercial print featuring a captive elephant painted pink, or donate proceeds to conservation.

articles.headline: Scientists Find One Reason Why Northwest India Floods More
articles.summary: Two subseasonal weather patterns, stronger monsoon oscillations and a slower mid-latitude oscillation, are making northwest India more flood-prone.

articles.headline: Tighter environmental regulations proposed for industrial zones
articles.summary: Environmental authorities have proposed stricter regulations for industrial zones to curb pollution and promote sustainable practices. Industry stakeholders are being consulted on the feasibility and potential economic impact of these new rules.

articles.headline: India updates national policy on climate change mitigation
articles.summary: India has revised its national policy on climate change mitigation and adaptation, setting more ambitious targets for reducing carbon emissions and promoting renewable energy sources.

articles.headline: Local communities participate in new water conservation projects
articles.summary: Several local communities are actively engaging in new water conservation initiatives, including rainwater harvesting and watershed management, to address water scarcity.

articles.headline: New conservation projects launched to protect biodiversity
articles.summary: Several new environmental conservation projects have been initiated across the country to protect endangered species and fragile ecosystems, promoting biodiversity.

articles.headline: Innovative waste management initiative launched in city suburbs
articles.summary: An innovative new waste management initiative, focusing on segregation and recycling, has been piloted in city suburbs, aiming to reduce landfill burden and promote sustainability.

articles.headline: Massive tree plantation drive initiated by local environmental groups
articles.summary: Local environmental groups have launched a massive tree plantation drive, aiming to increase green cover and improve air quality in urban and peri-urban areas.

articles.headline: Massive environmental cleanup drive targets city's waterfront
articles.summary: A large-scale environmental cleanup drive was organized along the city's waterfront, with volunteers and civic bodies working to remove waste and restore natural beauty.

articles.headline: Indian Refiners Procuring Iranian Crude, Centre Clarifies
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas has clarified that Indian refiners are procuring Iranian crude oil. The Centre dismissed reports of diverted tankers and stated there are no payment hurdles.

articles.headline: Trump Weighs Cabinet Shake-up Amid Iran War Fallout
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump is considering a cabinet shake-up due to frustration over the fallout from the war with Iran. This fallout has driven up gas prices and lowered his approval ratings.

articles.headline: Five Held for Blast Outside Punjab BJP Headquarters
articles.summary: Punjab Police have arrested five individuals in connection with a grenade blast outside the BJP office in Chandigarh. Weapons were recovered, and police claim the case is solved.

articles.headline: 'Custodial Death' of Youth Sparks Violence in Bihar; Police Suspended
articles.summary: The alleged custodial death of a youth in Katihar, Bihar, triggered mob violence. The Station House Officer and Investigating Officer have been suspended pending an inquiry.
